Gene_Accession_Number.reason: The Methods section explicitly states 'The full-length sequence of the PtrVINV2 gene (Potri.003G112600) was cloned'.

Source_Species.reason: The study material is Populus trichocarpa; title and Materials and methods specify P. trichocarpa genotype Nisqually-1.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Explicitly stated throughout the paper as Populus trichocarpa.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: Materials and methods explicitly names the genotype/variety Nisqually-1.

Applied_Species.reason: The experiments were conducted in poplar/Populus trichocarpa.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The transformed species is explicitly Populus trichocarpa.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: The applied experimental genotype is explicitly Nisqually-1.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Results directly measure and report changes in lignin, hemicellulose, sucrose, glucose and fructose in PtrVINV2 lines; cellulose was unchanged and is not included here.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: The paper explicitly classifies lignin/cellulose/hemicellulose as structural carbohydrates and sucrose/glucose/fructose as non-structural carbohydrates.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Results link PtrVINV2-associated metabolite changes to secondary xylem development and salt tolerance/osmotic regulation.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Structural/non-structural carbohydrate contents were measured in stems/developing xylem context, and salt-stress sugars were measured in leaves; the paper specifically mentions developing xylem, stem samples, and leaves under salt stress.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Summary and localization results identify PtrVINV2 as a vacuolar invertase (VINV), i.e., acid invertase family.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: Figure 1 and Results explicitly show PtrVINV2 localizes to the vacuolar membrane.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Results cite prior analysis (Figure S1; Zhang et al., 2023) showing PtrVINV2 transcript positively correlated with cellulose and negatively with hemicellulose, lignin and starch.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: qRT-PCR results show overexpression driven mainly in developing xylem with only 0.64- to 1.14-fold variation in leaves and roots.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results show knockout reduced lignin and increased hemicellulose without affecting cellulose, altered xylem development, and under salt stress knockout had higher sucrose and lower glucose/fructose with greater salt sensitivity; overexpression/knockout altered VINV activity accordingly.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are described in Results/Methods: developing xylem-specific overexpression, CRISPR knockout, qRT-PCR, onion epidermis localization, invertase assays, carbohydrate assays, staining, RNA-seq, WGCNA, and salt stress phenotyping.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Methods explicitly state DX15 (Potri.009G012200) developing xylem-specific promoter was used for overexpression.

Omics_Data.reason: RNA-seq on Illumina HiSeq and WGCNA are explicitly described; no accession is provided, so 'accession NA' is consistent with the paper text.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Results/Methods include developing xylem-specific overexpression and 200 mmol/L NaCl treatment for 10 days.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: These outcomes are reported: knockout lowered lignin, raised hemicellulose, reduced xylem width/cell layers, increased salt sensitivity; overexpression did not significantly change structural carbohydrate contents.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Results and Summary state cellulose remained unaffected; KO-PtrVINV2-18 showed dwarfism/chlorosis likely from T-DNA insertion effects and was excluded from later assays.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three summary points are directly supported by the paper's Results and Summary.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Summary explicitly says PtrVINV2 presents a promising candidate gene for breeding salt-tolerant poplar; modified wood properties are also supported by lignin/hemicellulose findings.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Non-structural carbohydrate association is inferable from the red module correlations, but not directly validated for this specific gene.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper cites prior literature calling Potri.001G219100 osmotic regulation-related and hypothesizes involvement in salt tolerance, but no direct functional test in this paper links it to osmotic regulation.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Results explicitly identify Potri.001G219100 as a MYB hub transcription factor in the red module.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: No subcellular localization for Potri.001G219100 is reported in this paper.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Results explicitly state Potri.001G219100 is a hub TF in the red module, and WGCNA shows the red module positively correlated with starch, sucrose and CWINV activity.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: The paper only proposes that this hub gene may participate in salt tolerance mechanisms; there is no direct perturbation or phenotype for Potri.001G219100.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Its identification in this paper is through WGCNA hub gene screening from RNA-seq data.

Omics_Data.reason: RNA-seq and WGCNA were used; no accession is given in the paper.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: It is discussed in the salt tolerance context, but the gene itself was not directly examined under salt treatment in Results.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper explicitly notes it is osmotic regulation-related based on prior literature and that no direct functional validation was done in this study.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each point matches the paper: identified as hub MYB in a red module correlated with starch/sucrose/CWINV, proposed role in PtrVINV2-associated salt tolerance, and no direct perturbation experiment.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper frames these hub genes as hypotheses/candidates, but does not explicitly propose Potri.001G219100 alone as a breeding marker/gene.
